channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Governo avalia incluir MEIs em negociação de endividados e restringir aposta em bets
item.description: The Lula government is studying an expansion of the new debt renegotiation program to include individual microentrepreneurs (MEIs) and impose conditions, such as restricting access to betting apps, for those receiving federal aid. The measure aims to broaden the reach of the debt relief policy while introducing conditionalities tied to beneficiaries’ financial behavior.

item.title: Rombo das estatais tornou-se rotina no governo Lula
item.description: In the first two months of 2026, federal state-owned enterprises recorded a deficit of R$ 4.16 billion, the worst result for the period since 2002 and R$ 2.8 billion higher than in the same period of 2025. The Lula government avoids the obvious privatization of Correios, and the worsening deficit trend may deepen the fiscal crisis, with public debt already at a record 79.2% of GDP.

item.title: Horizonte para mulheres é estreito na próxima eleição
item.description: The 2026 Brazilian presidential election is poised to be the first since 2006 without a female candidate at the top of the ticket, signaling a setback for women's representation after previous gains. Despite Dilma Rousseff's two victories and competitive runs by Marina Silva and Simone Tebet, female participation has succumbed to male dominance within parties, reflecting the failure of feminist demands for greater inclusion.

item.title: Por que alguns partidos recebem mais dinheiro do que outros?
item.description: Political party financing in Brazil depends on electoral performance, with funds from the Party Fund and Electoral Fund distributed based on votes and elected legislators. Parties with larger congressional delegations, such as PL and PT, receive more resources, while those failing the performance threshold lose access to funds and broadcast time. Since 2015, corporate donations have been banned, allowing only contributions from individuals.

item.title: Eleições 2026: partidos e federações que vão lançar candidatos têm até este sábado para obter registro no TSE
item.description: Parties and party federations planning to launch candidates in the October elections must register their statutes with the TSE by this Saturday (April 4), as required by law six months before the vote. Registration is necessary to access party funds, broadcast time, and exclusive use of name, acronym, and ballot number. Brazil currently has 30 registered parties and five official federations, including the newly approved União Progressista (União Brasil and PP), sanctioned on March 26.

item.title: Ao menos 17 ministros deixaram governo para concorrer às eleições; veja quem entra e quem sai
item.description: At least 17 ministers have left the government to run in the 2026 elections, complying with the legal desincompatibilization period. Geraldo Alckmin (PSB) was confirmed as Lula’s pre-candidate for vice president, while Gleisi Hoffmann (PT) is expected to run for Senate in Paraná and Rui Costa (PT) for Senate in Bahia. The reshuffle includes confirmed departures and new appointments such as Márcio Elias Rosa (Industry, Commerce and Services) and Miriam Belchior (Chief of Staff), with some positions still under interim leadership or awaiting announcement.

item.title: Após fiasco do Carnaval, Lula começa a ir à luta para ‘limpar’ imagem com conservadores
item.description: Following the backlash over the Rio Carnival homage, Lula is shifting his campaign to regain conservative voters — particularly low-income women — by emphasizing family-centered themes like income, time with children, and cost of living. The strategy aims to frame economic policies as tangible benefits, but it faces obstacles from inflation, recent communication missteps, and the perception that social programs are now seen as entitlements rather than government achievements.

item.title: Flávio pede fim das “picuinhas” para enfrentar “missão divina”
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro called for an end to internal "squabbles" to focus on the "divine mission" of his father, Jair Bolsonaro, thanking supporters who defend him from the "hate cabinet" of Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva. He emphasized that the defense stems from respect for the former president and an understanding of a higher purpose.

item.title: Flávio Bolsonaro viajou com família para Flórida e Rio em jatinhos emprestados por empresários
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro took at least two trips in 2025 with his family on private jets lent by businessmen, according to documents and reports gathered by Estadão. The travels included destinations such as Florida and Rio de Janeiro, raising concerns about potential undue advantages and transparency in the financing of personal expenses by supporters.

item.title: Novo marketing e velhos conflitos criam turbulências na campanha de Flávio Bolsonaro
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro's campaign seeks to rebrand him as a moderate candidate to broaden his appeal, but risks alienating his core bolsonarist base. While allies note early positive signals and his competitiveness in polls, critics warn that moderation could lose loyal voters and create openings for rivals like Ronaldo Caiado. The PL faces internal tensions, particularly over selecting a female running mate, as the campaign treats the candidate as a malleable 'political product' shaped by public reaction.

item.title: Com anistia e evangélicos, Caiado invade terreno de Flávio e tensiona disputa na direita
item.description: Ronaldo Caiado enters the 2026 presidential race as a PSD alternative to polarization, but his support for amnesty for those involved in the coup plot and his alignment with bolsonarismo create direct tension with Flávio Bolsonaro. His strategy targets conservative and evangelical voters, backed by Bishop Samuel Ferreira of the Assembleia de Deus Madureira, aiming to capitalize if Flávio weakens due to political wear. Caiado’s key challenge is differentiating himself from Flávio without losing the bolsonarist base — otherwise, voters will likely choose the more familiar name.

item.title: Trump vira sujeito oculto da disputa entre Lula e Flávio
item.description: Lula and Flávio Bolsonaro, as the protagonists of Brazilian polarization, have made Donald Trump a hidden subject in the 2026 presidential race, using his figure as a symbolic reference in the campaign. The 2026 election takes on the appearance of a rematch of 2022, with Trump serving as an ideological backdrop, even without direct involvement.

item.title: TSE tira cassação de Denarium da pauta
item.description: The TSE has added a case against Governor Antonio Denarium (PP) regarding 2022 election propaganda to its agenda, but not the cassation case, which remains pending. The included action led to a fine by the TRE-RR, while higher-impact cases — such as the expansion of the Cesta da Família program and use of social programs during election year — await judgment. The development occurs at the end of the party window, following pressure from indigenous leader Davi Kopenawa, who urged the TSE to rule on the cases on March 24.

item.title: Bolsonaro está em fase pré-operatória, com “limitação” do braço, diz relatório
item.description: The defense of former President Jair Bolsonaro submitted medical and physiotherapy reports to Supreme Court Minister Alexandre de Moraes, indicating Bolsonaro is in a pre-operative phase with arm limitation. He is currently under temporary humanitarian house arrest, as ordered by Moraes, who requires weekly medical updates on his condition.

item.title: Lula nomeia número 2 de Alckmin para comandar o Ministério da Indústria
item.description: Lula appointed Marcio Elias Rosa, executive secretary of the Ministry of Industry and Trade, as Geraldo Alckmin's replacement after Alckmin stepped down to comply with election ineligibility rules ahead of the 2026 reelection bid. Rosa, a long-time Alckmin aide and former São Paulo prosecutor specializing in collective rights and citizenship, holds a doctorate in state law from PUC-SP. The appointment was published in an extra edition of the Official Gazette.

item.title: Lula nomeia Márcio Elias Rosa como ministro da Indústria
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva appointed Márcio Elias Rosa as the new Minister of Development, Industry, Trade and Services, replacing Geraldo Alckmin, who is stepping down to run for vice president on Lula's re-election ticket. The appointment follows Márcio França's announcement of leaving the government to run in São Paulo's state elections, while his potential Senate candidacy on Fernando Haddad's ticket remains under discussion.

item.title: Com delação do fim do mundo, Vorcaro quer ‘fazer uma coisa positiva para o Brasil’
item.description: Daniel Vorcaro, owner of Banco Master, is organizing his plea bargain in blocks to present to Supreme Court Minister André Mendonça within 45 days. According to interlocutors, he aims to 'do something positive for Brazil' and has already disclosed details on bribe payments, Congress politicians, and potential Central Bank links, while indicating Lula and Flávio Bolsonaro are unlikely to be implicated. The testimony may involve STF ministers, and any omission risks voiding the agreement.

item.title: “Manda o nome agora, mesmo com risco de ser rejeitado”: o conselho dado a Lula na crise com Senado
item.description: President Lula decided to nominate Jorge Messias to the Supreme Federal Court despite the risk of Senate rejection, following advice to act now to avoid losing initiative. The government’s political articulation faces resistance, particularly from Senate President Davi Alcolumbre, who controls the agenda and could delay the hearing indefinitely. While the nomination may have positive electoral impact among religious voters, the risk of political blockade is high, with the possibility of postponement until after the 2026 elections.

item.title: Lula oficializa número 2 de Alckmin para comandar Ministério da Indústria
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva appointed Márcio Elias, the number two official at the Ministry of Development, Industry, Commerce and Services, as the new Minister of Industry, following the removal of Geraldo Alckmin to run in this year's municipal elections. The appointment comes as the government prepares for the 2026 elections, with Alckmin seeking a return to the São Paulo gubernatorial race.

item.title: Número 2 assume lugar de Alckmin no comando do Ministério da Indústria
item.description: Marcio Elias Rosa, the number two official at the Ministry of Industry and Commerce, will assume leadership of the ministry in place of Vice President Geraldo Alckmin, due to electoral law requiring public officials to resign six months before elections. Rosa, Alckmin’s trusted aide and former executive secretary, has a background in the São Paulo Public Prosecutor’s Office and played a key role in negotiating U.S. tariffs on Brazilian goods under the Trump administration. He has accompanied President Lula on all international trips over the past three years, including the October meeting with Trump in Malaysia.

item.title: Messias avança na CCJ, mas ainda não tem votos para aprovação ao STF; acompanhe o placar do Estadão
item.description: The nomination of Jorge Messias to the STF has advanced in the Senate's CCJ, with nine favorable votes, but still needs five more to reach the required 14. Senate President Davi Alcolumbre, initially resistant, appears to have acquiesced, though the outcome remains uncertain. The current tally shows eight opposed, one undecided, and six senators who did not take a position.

item.title: Atriz ironiza picanha de Lula: “O Diabo sempre usou comida”
item.description: Actress Lidi Lisboa criticized the Lula government by associating the promise of 'cheap picanha' with the devil's strategy of deceiving people through food, citing biblical examples like the apple in Eden and bread in the desert. She did not name names directly, but the reference to picanha alludes to Lula's 2022 campaign promise. Known for playing Jezabel in a Record TV telenovela, she used religious symbolism to question Brazil's tax system and political landscape.

item.title: Cabo Daciolo se filia a partido e confirma pré-candidatura à Presidência
item.description: Former federal deputy Cabo Daciolo announced his affiliation with the Mobilização Nacional party and confirmed his pre-candidacy for the Brazilian presidency in the 2026 elections. He had previously announced his intention to run in 2022 but withdrew to support Ciro Gomes. On social media, he criticizes both Lula and Bolsonaro for insufficient investment in national infrastructure, particularly the BR-319 highway and the Madeira-Mamoré railway.

item.title: Planalto oficializa exonerações de Geraldo Alckmin e Gleisi Hoffmann
item.description: The Planalto officially announced the resignations of Ministers Geraldo Alckmin and Gleisi Hoffmann so they can run in the October elections, complying with the legal disassociation deadline. Alckmin remains vice-president and is seeking re-election on Lula's ticket, while Hoffmann aims to run for the Senate from Paraná. Fifteen other ministers also left the government this week, with vacancies still unfilled in the MDIC, Institutional Relations Secretariat, and Entrepreneurship Ministry.

item.title: A batalha de Minas: por que Lula aposta tudo em Pacheco
item.description: The PT is betting on Rodrigo Pacheco's pre-candidacy for governor of Minas Gerais to reduce its rejection in the state and sustain Lula's electoral base in 2026, as Minas Gerais is decisive in determining the presidential election winner. Pacheco, seen as a moderate figure unassociated with traditional PT identity, aims to broaden appeal across wider elector segments, particularly through his fiscal record, while analysts note the mineiro landscape remains open and shaped by local agendas beyond national polarization.

item.title: Petrobras reafirma política de preços de combustíveis em comunicado
item.description: Petrobras reaffirmed its fuel pricing policy in a communication to the CVM, emphasizing that it continues to avoid immediately passing on international oil price volatility to domestic markets, despite recent global price increases driven by Middle Eastern geopolitical tensions. The company stated that price adjustments are based on technical analysis and internal governance, and that the communication was a response to CVM inquiries regarding potential domestic-international price gaps that could result in financial losses for the firm.

item.title: Graeml ingressa no PSD de Ratinho Jr. e mantém apoio a Flávio
item.description: Journalist Cristina Graeml officially joined the PSD in Paraná, responding to an invitation from Governor Ratinho Júnior. She reiterated her support for Flávio Bolsonaro as her presidential pre-candidate, prompting requests from PL members for her to leave the party. The move highlights tensions between party alliances and support for radical right-wing figures ahead of the 2026 elections.

item.title: Advogado que pediu pausa para jantar a Moraes é pré-candidato a deputado
item.description: Lawyer Matheus Milanez, part of the defense team for General Augusto Heleno, has been announced as a pre-candidate for federal deputy from the Federal District by the Republicans party. His affiliation was shared by party president Joaquim Mauro on social media. Milanez went viral in June 2025 during the coup plot trial after requesting a delay in testimony to have dinner, prompting an ironic response from Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes, which drew laughter in the courtroom.

item.title: Tarcísio sanciona aumento salarial de 10% para polícias de SP
item.description: Governor Tarcísio de Freitas (Republicanos) of São Paulo signed a law granting a 10% linear salary increase for the Civil, Military, and Technical-Scientific police forces. The adjustment takes effect upon publication in the State Official Gazette and aims to valorize public security personnel.

item.title: PT discute plano B para 2026 e admite risco de Lula ficar fora da disputa
item.description: The PT is discussing a 'plan B' for 2026 due to Flávio Bolsonaro's rising poll numbers and increasing rejection of Lula, raising the possibility that the president may not run. Camilo Santana and Fernando Haddad are being tested as potential successors, with Haddad seen as a more immediate alternative due to similar voting intent and lower rejection than Lula, while Santana is viewed as a medium-term project. The debate highlights the PT's structural difficulty in renewing leadership, with some party factions resisting Haddad for lacking sufficient combativeness.

item.title: Moraes vota para manter Filipe Martins preso em Ponta Grossa
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes voted to keep Filipe Martins, former aide to Jair Bolsonaro, detained in Ponta Grossa Prison, rejecting the defense's request to transfer him to a medical penal facility. Martins was sentenced to 21 years for his role in the attempted coup and is barred from giving interviews due to concerns about potential unrest. The First Chamber of the STF is still reviewing the appeal, with Moraes’ vote recorded and three other justices set to rule by April 13.

item.title: Mendonça desobriga Ibaneis de ir à CPI do Crime Organizado
item.description: Supreme Court Justice André Mendonça ruled that former Distrito Federal governor Ibaneis Rocha is not required to appear before the Organized Crime CPI, though he may choose to attend and remain silent, with legal counsel and no obligation to tell the truth. The ruling is based on the right against self-incrimination, as the committee treats Ibaneis as an investigant due to alleged links between his law firm and investigations like Compliance Zero and Carbono Oculto, plus his role in BRB decisions involving Banco Master, which was liquidated in 2025 over fraud suspicions. Ibaneis and Cláudio Castro were formally summoned on March 31 after failing to respond to earlier invitations in December 2025 and February 2026.

item.title: Governo faz apuração sobre prática abusiva em leilão de gás de cozinha
item.description: The Ministry of Mines and Energy has requested the National Consumer Secretariat (Senacon) to evaluate potential abusive practices in the commercialization of cooking gas (GLP) in Brazil, following auctions with markups exceeding 100% above official prices. President Lula denounced the auctions as 'cretinice,' stating they were conducted against Petrobras and government guidance, and vowed to annul them to shield low-income consumers from price hikes linked to international tensions.

item.title: Em entraves com Congresso, Lula deixa articulaçăo do governo com interino
item.description: President Lula is facing challenges in his relationship with Congress following the departure of Minister Gleisi Hoffmann from the Institutional Relations Secretariat, creating uncertainty over who will lead the government's political articulation. The position, vital for negotiating with the legislature, is currently filled by an interim official as the administration seeks to navigate legislative obstacles.

item.title: Tarcísio vai conceder para iniciativa privada 6 parques na capital paulista; veja quais
item.description: The government of Tarcísio de Freitas published in the State Official Gazette the administrative concession notice for six parks in the state capital to private initiative. The measure aims to transfer the management of public spaces to the private sector under a public-private partnership regime. The specific parks involved were not specified in the provided content.

item.title: Mendonça desobriga Ibaneis de depor na CPI do Crime Organizado
item.description: Supreme Court Justice André Mendonça ruled that former Federal District Governor Ibaneis Rocha is not compelled to testify before the Organized Crime Parliamentary Inquiry Committee (CPI), granting his lawyers’ request. The decision upholds the right against self-incrimination and human dignity, allowing Ibaneis to attend or remain silent, be accompanied by counsel, and face no penalties for non-appearance. The CPI sought testimony regarding the failed attempt by the Brasília Regional Bank to acquire Banco Master, which was blocked by the Central Bank in 2025 due to financial fraud.

item.title: Prefeito ‘tiktoker’ de Sorocaba decide não se candidatar em 2026
item.description: Sorocaba's mayor Rodrigo Manga (Republicanos) announced he will not run in 2026 and will remain in office. He returned to City Hall on April 1 after Supreme Court Justice Kassio Nunes Marques suspended his removal. The decision was shared in a video posted on his social media.

item.title: Tarcísio sanciona lei que aumenta em 10% o salário das polícias de SP
item.description: Governor Tarcísio de Freitas (Republicanos) signed a law granting a 10% salary increase for São Paulo’s Civil, Military, and Technical-Scientific police forces, benefiting approximately 198,000 active and retired employees, with an estimated fiscal impact of R$1.75 billion. The measure was approved urgently in the Alesp to meet the electoral deadline, as Bill 226/2026 required sanction by April 6 to take effect in the October elections. The Prison Police, under the SAP, were excluded from the raise.

item.title: Racha no clã Bolsonaro expõe disputa pelo espólio político e preocupa campanha de Flávio
item.description: The internal struggle over Jair Bolsonaro’s political legacy has intensified following his withdrawal from active politics, with Flávio Bolsonaro, Michelle Bolsonaro, and other family members vying for control of the bolsonarist movement. Flávio’s presidential candidacy, launched without family consensus, has exacerbated tensions, particularly with Michelle, whose strategic role in PL Mulher and outreach to female voters has been sidelined. Analysts warn that this familial division may undermine Flávio’s campaign cohesion and structured agenda, despite his rising poll numbers, increasing electoral vulnerability.

item.title: Bancada evangélica mais alinhada a bolsonarismo ajuda Flávio e desafia Lula
item.description: The evangelical caucus in Congress strengthened Bolsonaro's PL party after the partisan window, in a move that supports Flávio Bolsonaro's pre-candidacy and challenges Lula's efforts toapproach the evangelical segment.

item.title: Tarcísio: PM preso por feminicídio deve apodrecer na cadeia
item.description: São Paulo Governor Tarcísio de Freitas stated that the military police lieutenant colonel detained on suspicion of femicide should 'rot in prison' and has been removed from the force. The victim, military police officer Gisele Alves Santana, was found dead in February, with the case initially treated as suicide but later reclassified as qualified femicide after forensic evidence indicated assault and crime scene tampering.

item.title: Brasileiros lideram ranking de turistas barrados em Portugal
item.description: In 2025, Brazilians were the largest group of tourists denied entry into Portugal, with 749 cases out of 2,140 total foreign nationals barred — despite a 50% decrease from 2024. The main reasons were lack of documentation, insufficient means of subsistence, and invalid visas. The total number of foreigners denied entry rose 23% compared to 2024, reaching 2,100.

item.title: Lula decide enviar PL sobre fim da 6x1 com urgência ao Congresso
item.description: The Lula government has decided to send to Congress a bill with constitutional urgency to end the 6×1 work schedule, proposing a 5×2 workweek, reducing weekly hours from 44 to 40, and prohibiting wage reductions. The move aims to accelerate approval in the first semester and will be used as a campaign slogan for re-election, with a major workers' event planned for May 1 led by Guilherme Boulos.

item.title: Lula recua de plano para compra de avião presidencial por temor de desgaste eleitoral
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva has backed off plans to purchase a new presidential aircraft due to fears of electoral backlash ahead of the 2026 campaign. Although budgets ranging from R$1.4 billion to R$2 billion were prepared by the Defense Ministry and Aeronautics, the process was halted to avoid negative political impact in the year he seeks a fourth term. The decision stems from high costs, limited international availability of such aircraft, and the current presidential plane (Aerolula) operating with a leased turbine since a 2024 incident in Mexico.

item.title: Augusto Aras lança relato sobre como o MP atuou na pandemia
item.description: Former Prosecutor General Augusto Aras shared a Federal Public Ministry document detailing the MP's actions during the Covid-19 pandemic, titled 'Ações que salvam'. He emphasized the collective effort of prosecutors and promoters and posted an image claiming the STF archived investigations initiated by the PGR, dismissing other allegations as 'fake news'.

item.title: Tarcísio diz esperar que tenente-coronel acusado de feminicídio ‘apodreça na cadeia’
item.description: São Paulo Governor Tarcísio de Freitas demanded an exemplary punishment for PM Lieutenant Colonel Geraldo Leite Rosa Neto, accused of femicide against his wife, soldier Gisele Alves Santana, stating he should 'rot in prison'. The statement came the same day the PM transferred him to reserve with full salary, despite his preventive detention and charges of fraud and crime scene tampering. The investigation reveals a pattern of domestic violence, with messages from the victim showing a macho, aggressive, possessive, manipulative, and authoritarian behavior.

item.title: Em busca do passado perdido
item.description: The officialization of the Lula/Alckmin ticket in 2026 is portrayed as a dull electoral strategy aiming to replicate the 2022 model, reviving the 'democracy versus coup' narrative and the 'democratic front against fascism' framing. The critique suggests this approach reflects a lack of innovative alternatives by the PT, relying on a worn-out discourse to mobilize its base.

item.title: Pesquisas eleitorais acendem alerta no Planalto com ‘surpresa’ de Flávio Bolsonaro
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro’s rise in polling, reaching a technical tie with Lula in the first round, has triggered alarm in the Planalto and underscored an increasingly polarized and unpredictable election. Experts note that while his surge surprised even seasoned analysts, it reflects similarly high rejection levels among top candidates, alongside external variables like economic crises, potential strikes, and unresolved state-level races. The government struggles to respond, misdiagnosing Lula’s electoral weakness as a communication issue when it is rooted in voter concerns over cost of living, inflation, and public safety.

item.title: Cabo Daciolo se filia ao Mobiliza, lança candidatura à Presidência e anuncia Ricardo Rocha como ministro dos Esportes
item.description: Former federal deputy and evangelical pastor Cabo Daciolo affiliated with the Mobiliza party and launched his 2026 presidential candidacy. During the announcement, he named former Brazilian national team defender Ricardo Rocha as his proposed Minister of Sports, emphasizing his nationalist and Christian platform. Daciolo, who received 1.26% of the vote in 2018, is again seeking evangelical support in a race contested by Flávio Bolsonaro and Lula.

item.title: Saídas de Gleisi e Alckmin de ministérios são oficializadas por Lula
item.description: President Lula officially approved the resignations of Minister Gleisi Hoffmann and Vice President Geraldo Alckmin from their government positions so they can run in the 2026 elections. Gleisi is running for Senate in Paraná, while Alckmin seeks re-election as vice president on Lula’s ticket. The resignations comply with electoral law requiring officials to leave office six months before the election, with the deadline ending this Saturday (April 4), and join at least 15 other ministers pursuing elective offices this year.
